pattern: Two linear equations in two unknowns: 4j + 2s = tomato and j + s = cheese. Solving gives j = (tomato - 2*cheese) / 2. The solution is valid only when that quantity is a non-negative integer and s = cheese - j is also non-negative.
hints: Write two equations: one for tomato slices (jumbo uses 4, small uses 2) and one for cheese slices (each burger uses exactly 1). You have two unknowns and two equations.
Eliminate s by substitution: from the cheese equation s = cheese - j; substitute into the tomato equation to get j in closed form.
Check divisibility and non-negativity before returning. If (tomato - 2*cheese) is negative, odd, or produces a negative s, return an empty list.

edge_inputs.description: both inputs zero, zero burgers of each type
tomato too small relative to cheese, j would be negative
tomato is zero but cheese is nonzero

stress.description: random valid (j,s) pairs scaled to max range — verifies round-trip correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    j = rng.randint(0, 2500000)
    s = rng.randint(0, 10000000 - j)
    tomato = 4 * j + 2 * s
    cheese = j + s
    return [tomato, cheese]
